title: Why “御殿場市” is trending in JP: 20,000+ searches, the facts (July 19, 2026)
description: “御殿場市” is trending in JP with 20,000+ searches reported by Google. The verified coverage behind the spike, from NHKニュース and 2 more.

sources.title: 【続報】御殿場のサッカー場で倒木 小学生の試合を観戦 幼児から40代女性まで7人搬送 うち1人は長期入院が必要な重症
木が倒れ子ども含む複数人搬送 静岡 御殿場
静岡のサッカーグラウンドに高さ20mの木が倒れる 観戦者7人搬送 [静岡県]

sections.heading: What is trending: “御殿場市”
paragraphs: “御殿場市” appeared on Google Trends' Trending Now list for JP (snapshot taken 2026-07-19T08:34:38+00:00 UTC). Google reports at least 20,000 searches for this term — that figure is a lower bound published in the official Trends feed, not an exact count.

sections.heading: What's behind the spike — the coverage
paragraphs: The Trends feed associates this search spike with 3 news stories from NHKニュース, Yahoo!ニュース, 朝日新聞. The headlines, exactly as published:
list: “【続報】御殿場のサッカー場で倒木 小学生の試合を観戦 幼児から40代女性まで7人搬送 うち1人は長期入院が必要な重症” — Yahoo!ニュース
“木が倒れ子ども含む複数人搬送 静岡 御殿場” — NHKニュース
“静岡のサッカーグラウンドに高さ20mの木が倒れる 観戦者7人搬送 [静岡県]” — 朝日新聞

sections.heading: Timeline
paragraphs: The trend entry was published to the Google Trends feed at Sun, 19 Jul 2026 00:50:00 -0700. Our snapshot was taken at 2026-07-19T08:34:38+00:00 UTC and this briefing was assembled at 2026-07-19T08:34:39+00:00 UTC.

sections.heading: How to read these numbers
paragraphs: Google Trends reports approximate search volume as a lower bound (for example “20K+”). Actual volume can be significantly higher. Trending status reflects acceleration in searches, not the absolute size of a topic. For the full story behind the spike, read the cited coverage below — this briefing intentionally adds no speculation beyond the sourced facts.

faq.a: Google's Trending Now feed links the spike to coverage from NHKニュース, Yahoo!ニュース, 朝日新聞. The associated headlines are: “【続報】御殿場のサッカー場で倒木 小学生の試合を観戦 幼児から40代女性まで7人搬送 うち1人は長期入院が必要な重症” (Yahoo!ニュース); “木が倒れ子ども含む複数人搬送 静岡 御殿場” (NHKニュース); “静岡のサッカーグラウンドに高さ20mの木が倒れる 観戦者7人搬送 [静岡県]” (朝日新聞).

faq.q: How many people searched for “御殿場市”?
faq.a: Google reports at least 20,000 searches in JP as of the feed snapshot. This is a published lower bound, not an exact count.
This snapshot covers JP. The same term may trend differently in other regions.
